symptoms: Leaves have an even green color and normal shape.
No visible lesions, yellowing, or fungal growth.
treatment: Continue routine crop monitoring and balanced nutrition.
prevention: Use clean tools and disease-free planting material.
Maintain appropriate watering, spacing, and soil fertility.

symptoms: Older leaves develop brown spots with concentric rings.
Affected leaves may yellow and drop prematurely.
treatment: Remove and dispose of heavily infected leaves.
Apply a locally approved fungicide according to its label.
prevention: Rotate crops and remove infected plant debris.
Water at soil level and improve airflow between plants.

symptoms: Leaves develop rapidly expanding, water-soaked dark lesions.
White fungal growth may appear on leaf undersides in humid weather.
treatment: Remove infected plants promptly to reduce spread.
Consult local guidance for an approved late-blight fungicide program.
prevention: Use resistant varieties and certified disease-free plants.
Avoid overhead irrigation and monitor during cool, wet conditions.

symptoms: Leaves are evenly green with normal growth and texture.
No visible spots, lesions, wilting, or abnormal discoloration.
treatment: Continue routine crop monitoring and balanced nutrition.
prevention: Maintain good field sanitation, drainage, and crop rotation.

symptoms: Older leaves show dark brown lesions with concentric rings.
Leaf tissue around lesions may become yellow.
treatment: Remove severely affected foliage where practical.
Apply a locally approved fungicide according to its label.
prevention: Rotate away from solanaceous crops and remove crop debris.
Reduce plant stress through appropriate irrigation and nutrition.

symptoms: Leaves develop irregular, water-soaked lesions that turn brown or black.
White fungal growth may occur around lesion margins in humid conditions.
treatment: Destroy infected foliage and volunteer plants promptly.
Follow local agricultural guidance for approved fungicide use.
prevention: Use certified seed and resistant varieties when available.
Avoid prolonged leaf wetness and monitor forecasts for blight risk.
